rawContent.message: No previous snapshot found — first QA run after developer change

build.details: taktician_validate_all passed. Build and lint clean for WideSocialMedia. Global lint warnings in unrelated blocks (Accordion, Archive, ContentWithMedia, etc.) are pre-existing and not caused by this change.

lint.details: No lint errors in WideSocialMedia block files. Pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in other blocks are unrelated.

registration.details: block.json registration valid. apiVersion 3, takt/wide-social-media-item name correct, file references resolve, childBlocks valid, attribute types correct.

issues.detail: iconSlug defined in block.json as type:string with no default (correct — undefined means not set)

EVERY item on the test page uses logoId:195 only — no item uses iconSlug. The primary new feature (IconPicker with iconSlug) is not exercised on the test page at the frontend PHP rendering level. The iconSlug path in PHP (file_get_contents from resources/) is never invoked by any test page item.

tsxPhpSync.details: taktician_validate_sync passed with only expected warnings. Root wrapper mismatches (TSX uses BlockControls/li wrapper vs PHP li) are correct — the automated tool sees BlockControls as the outermost TSX element which is a Gutenberg editor-only wrapper, not a frontend element. PHP correctly renders <li> as root frontend element. phpExtractPattern pass, phpDocblock pass, attributeUsage pass, contextConsistency pass, phpFunctionExistence pass.

syncDetails.phpDocblock: pass — @var annotations present for all attributes
syncDetails.attributeUsage: pass — iconSlug and logoId used consistently in TSX and PHP
syncDetails.contextConsistency: pass — usesContext takt/wide-social-media/showLabel matches PHP $block->context usage

iconSlugAttribute.detail: iconSlug attribute present in /var/www/ign/wp-content/themes/ign/blocks/WideSocialMedia/WideSocialMediaItem/block.json line 27, type:string, no default.

tsxImports.detail: IconPicker and SuggestedIcon imported from @taktdev/components on line 13 of WideSocialMediaItem.tsx. Matches FooterInfoSocialItem.tsx pattern exactly.

svgImports.detail: All 12 ReactComponent SVG imports present in WideSocialMediaItem.tsx: IconBluesky, IconFacebookAlt, IconFacebook, IconImdb, IconInstagram, IconLinkedin, IconPinterest, IconThreads, IconTiktok, IconVimeo, IconX, IconYoutube (lines 15-26).

suggestedIconsArray.detail: suggestedIcons array has exactly 12 entries matching FooterInfoSocialItem: bluesky, facebook, facebook-alt, imdb, instagram, linkedin, pinterest, threads, tiktok, vimeo, x, youtube. Labels match: Bluesky, Facebook, Facebook, IMDb, Instagram, LinkedIn, Pinterest, Threads, TikTok, Vimeo, X, YouTube.

iconPickerOnChange.detail: onChange handler at TSX line 125-130 sets iconSlug: icon?.slug ?? undefined and logoId: icon?.mediaId ?? undefined. This correctly clears the other field when switching between slug-based and media-based icons, and sets both to undefined when cleared.

phpIconSlugPriority.detail: PHP correctly prioritizes iconSlug over logoId. Lines 34-42: if (!empty($iconSlug)) reads SVG from resources/ folder, elseif (!empty($logoId)) falls back to theme_output_svg_or_img. Priority order is correct.

issues.check: PHP strips XML declaration from resource SVGs
issues.detail: PHP at line 38 uses preg_replace('/<\?xml.*?\?>\s*/', '', $iconHtml) which correctly strips the XML declaration from SVG files read via file_get_contents. The resource SVG files (e.g. icon-instagram.svg) do NOT have XML declarations — they start directly with <svg>, so no stripping is needed.

issues.check: XML declaration in logoId path output
issues.detail: The logoId fallback path uses theme_output_svg_or_img($logoId, false) which returns SVG file content from the WordPress media library. These SVGs contain an XML declaration (<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>). This declaration appears in the rendered HTML as <!--?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?--> (escaped by browser). 31 of 31 icon spans on the test page show this artifact. The PHP does NOT strip the XML declaration from the logoId path — only the iconSlug path strips it. This is a pre-existing issue with theme_output_svg_or_img and is not a regression of the current change, but is observable in rendered output.

phpLogoIdFallback.detail: PHP correctly falls back to theme_output_svg_or_img($logoId, false) when iconSlug is empty and logoId is set. All 9 test page variations confirm this path works — icons render (as SVG from media library, with XML declaration artifact noted above).

svgResourceFiles.detail: All 12 SVG resource files exist in /var/www/ign/wp-content/themes/ign/blocks/WideSocialMedia/WideSocialMediaItem/resources/: icon-bluesky.svg, icon-facebook.svg, icon-facebook-alt.svg, icon-imdb.svg, icon-instagram.svg, icon-linkedin.svg, icon-pinterest.svg, icon-threads.svg, icon-tiktok.svg, icon-vimeo.svg, icon-x.svg, icon-youtube.svg. None have XML declarations — they start directly with <svg>.

testPageRendering.details: Page renders correctly at all breakpoints (375, 768, 1440px). 9 block instances found matching expected 9 variations. All items in DemoContainers. No stale markup or placeholders. Icon spans (.w-6.h-6.shrink-0) present in all 9 sections. Label spans (text-xs) present in sections with showLabel=true (sections 0-5, 8) and absent in sections with showLabel=false (sections 6-7). Anchor links present in all sections. Page structure: headings correct, showLabel:false hides labels correctly.

specCompliance.detail: The test page does not exercise the new iconSlug feature at all. All 9 test page block variations use logoId:195 only — none use iconSlug. The developer should add at least one test page variation that uses iconSlug (e.g. iconSlug:'instagram') to verify the iconSlug PHP rendering path on the frontend. The PHP code for iconSlug is correct by code review, but it cannot be verified via page audit without a live example.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements — block is a static display element (social media links list)

accessibility.details: Links have aria-label from link.label field. Screen-reader text '(opens in new tab)' is rendered in sr-only span for all external links. Title attribute added to icon links when showLabel=false for tooltip. No keyboard interactive elements requiring ARIA pattern testing.

markupDuplication.details: No duplicated markup for responsive breakpoints found. Single DOM tree for both mobile and desktop.

issues.description: Test page has no iconSlug examples — the primary new feature is untested at the frontend PHP rendering level. All 9 variations use logoId:195 only. The iconSlug code path in PHP (reading SVG from resources/ directory) is never executed on the test page.
issues.fix: Update the test page to include at least one takt/wide-social-media-item with iconSlug set (e.g. {"iconSlug":"instagram","link":{"url":"https://instagram.com",...}}). Ideally add a full demo container showing all 12 icon slugs. Remove logoId from those items so the iconSlug path is exclusively exercised.

issues.description: XML declaration from media library SVGs appears as <!--?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?--> in rendered HTML when using the logoId fallback path. This is output by theme_output_svg_or_img() which reads SVG files from the WordPress media library that include an XML declaration. The browser treats the XML PI as invalid HTML and converts it to a comment. This is a pre-existing behavior of theme_output_svg_or_img and is not a regression of the iconSlug change. The iconSlug path (resource SVGs) does not have this issue as those files have no XML declaration.
issues.fix: This is a pre-existing issue with theme_output_svg_or_img. Not introduced by this change. If a fix is desired, the PHP could strip the XML declaration from the logoId path output too: $iconHtml = preg_replace('/<\?xml.*?\?>\s*/', '', $iconHtml); after the theme_output_svg_or_img call. However, since this is outside scope of the current change and the resource SVGs don't have this issue, this is informational only.

issues.description: iconSlug attribute in block.json has no default value (undefined). The logoId attribute has default:null. This is an inconsistency — both attributes should have the same default behavior. PHP uses !empty() which handles both undefined and null consistently, so this is functional, but block.json could be more explicit.
issues.fix: Consider adding "default": null to iconSlug for consistency with logoId: "iconSlug": { "type": "string", "default": null }. Not strictly required — empty check works correctly either way.

passedChecks: Build passes — no TypeScript or compilation errors
iconSlug attribute defined in block.json as type:string
TSX imports IconPicker and SuggestedIcon from @taktdev/components
All 12 SVG ReactComponent imports present in WideSocialMediaItem.tsx
suggestedIcons array has exactly 12 entries matching FooterInfoSocialItem exactly
IconPicker onChange handler sets iconSlug and logoId correctly (clears other field)
PHP prioritizes iconSlug over logoId (if/elseif structure)
PHP uses sanitize_file_name() on iconSlug before building file path (security correct)
PHP checks file_exists() before file_get_contents() (safe)
PHP uses preg_replace to strip XML declaration from resource SVGs (correct — resource SVGs don't have XML decls so no-op, but logic is correct)
PHP falls back to theme_output_svg_or_img when only logoId is set (backward compat verified on all 9 test page variations)
All 12 SVG resource files exist in WideSocialMediaItem/resources/
No SVG resource files contain XML declarations (they start directly with <svg>)
phpExtractPattern pass — no $attributes['key'] anti-pattern
phpDocblock pass — @var annotations for all attributes present
contextConsistency pass — usesContext/providesContext consistent
phpFunctionExistence pass — all called PHP functions exist
Test page has 9 block instances, all in DemoContainers, no stale markup
showLabel context correctly hides/shows labels on frontend (sections 6 and 7 have no label spans)
Icon spans present in all 9 sections
SR-only (opens in new tab) text present for all external links
Page renders at 375px, 768px, 1440px without errors
